steps.title: Use the graph-choice shortcut
steps.content: This is a perfect Desmos answer-choice check. Graph the four answer choices and compare them with the line in the question. The correct line must have the same intercepts as the shown graph.

steps.title: Read the intercepts
steps.content: The shown line crosses the $x$-axis at $(3,0)$ and the $y$-axis at $(0,-5)$. Choice A, $5x-3y=15$, has those same intercepts: setting $y=0$ gives $x=3$, and setting $x=0$ gives $y=-5$.

steps.title: Eliminate the other equations
steps.content: Choice B has the signs reversed, so its intercepts are $(-3,0)$ and $(0,5)$. Choices C and D swap the intercept sizes because the coefficients of $x$ and $y$ are reversed. Only choice A matches the graph.
